records.ety: [OF. receveir, recevoir, F. recevoir, fr. L. recipere; pref. re- re- + capere to take, seize. See Capable, Heave, and cf. Receipt, Reception, Recipe.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Received (r*sēvd"); p. pr. & vb. n. Receiving.]
usage: -- Receive, Accept. To receive describes simply the act of taking. To accept denotes the taking with approval, or for the purposes for which a thing is offered. Thus, we receive a letter when it comes to hand; we receive news when it reaches us; we accept a present when it is offered; we accept an invitation to dine with a friend.

defs: To take, as something that is offered, given, committed, sent, paid, or the like; to accept; as, to receive money offered in payment of a debt; to receive a gift, a message, or a letter.
quotes: Receyven all in gree that God us sent.

defs: Hence: To gain the knowledge of; to take into the mind by assent to; to give admission to; to accept, as an opinion, notion, etc.; to embrace.
quotes: The idea of solidity we receive by our touch.

defs: To allow, as a custom, tradition, or the like; to give credence or acceptance to.
quotes: Many other things there be which they have received to hold, as the washing of cups, and pots.

defs: To give admittance to; to permit to enter, as into one's house, presence, company, and the like; as, to receive a lodger, visitor, ambassador, messenger, etc.
quotes: They kindled a fire, and received us every one.

defs: To admit; to take in; to hold; to contain; to have capacity for; to be able to take in.
quotes: The brazen altar that was before the Lord was too little to receive the burnt offerings.

defs: To be affected by something; to suffer; to be subjected to; as, to receive pleasure or pain; to receive a wound or a blow; to receive damage.
quotes: Against his will he can receive no harm.

defs: To take from a thief, as goods known to be stolen.
To bat back (the ball) when served.
quotes: Who, if we knew What we receive, would either not accept Life offered, or soon beg to lay it down.

defs: To receive visitors; to be at home to receive calls; as, she receives on Tuesdays.
To return, or bat back, the ball when served; as, it is your turn to receive.
